stat_of_week: 40 - Sugar Land Skeeters scored 40 runs this week, the most by any team in a single week this season, powering an 8-1 demolition of Hiroshima Radiation
thriller_note: BaseballPro77 survived on the strength of pitching and stolen bases despite a weak offensive showing — only 2 HR and a .277 OBP — while Benchwarmers' Corbin Carroll (2 HR, 8 RBI, .500 OBP) couldn't quite carry the team over the line, leaving both squads in a precarious position as their rosters strain to find consistency.
key_storyline: Wisconsin Rapids Reapers delivered the week's most dominant performance, sweeping Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs 9-0 behind Ben Rice's monster week (3 HR, 9 RBI, .548 OBP), Mason Miller's filthy 0.30 WHIP relief work, and a team WHIP of 0.84 — yet the Reapers enter this recap still fighting for legitimacy in the power rankings after a slow start. Meanwhile, Wonder Wharf posted a goose egg in category wins despite having three quality starts and a respectable ERA, exposing a near-total offensive collapse that threatens to derail what was once a promising pitching-first strategy. The 9-0 scoreline is the most lopsided result of the season so far and raises serious questions about the Wonderdogs' floor.
lucky_reason: Benchwarmers won despite bottom-half marks in HR, strikeouts, and IP, posting a luck delta of +0.58 — the largest in the league this week — meaning their 5-4 loss result still outperformed what their underlying category stats deserved against most opponents.
unlucky_reason: General Hospital posted a stellar 2.10 ERA, 1.02 WHIP, and a .363 OBP while winning their matchup 6-4, yet their luck delta of -0.52 reveals they are one of the two most statistically underrewarded teams in the league — their category production should be generating far more wins on a neutral schedule.
records_broken: The lowest ERA by a winning team record was tied: General Hospital posted a 2.10 ERA this week, matching their own previous mark set in Week 2 (2.10 ERA, General Hospital). Additionally, Abbotsford-Colby Twins matched the all-time single-week strikeout record with 77 K, equaling the mark they themselves set in Week 2.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | League-best 40 R, 8-1 blowout, Drake Baldwin and Matt Olson both erupted — three straight top-tier weeks cements top spot | same
2. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | 9-0 sweep is the statement of the season; Ben Rice, Mason Miller, and a 0.84 WHIP rotation demand respect | up
3. General Hospital | 2.10 ERA, .363 OBP, and a 6-4 win despite -0.52 luck delta — this team is quietly undervalued and primed to surge | same
4. BaseballPro77 | Ugly offensive week (2 HR, .277 OBP) but pitching bailed them out; a 5-4 win is a 5-4 win and they remain elite on the mound | down
5. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Yordan Alvarez went nuclear (.600 OBP, 3 HR, 8 RBI), league-record-tying 77 K, and a 2.25 ERA — Merrill Kelly add could be pivotal | up
6. North Correa | Oneil Cruz (4 HR, 10 RBI) and Ohtani (.441 OBP) powered a 7-3 win; pitching ERA of 2.85 rounds out a balanced roster | same
7. Riverside Roughnecks | 38 runs, 10 HR, and a 7-3 victory — this team has quietly become one of the most explosive offenses in the league | up
8. Athletic Park Dragons | Lost 6-4 despite Judge, Wood, and Torres all contributing — thin pitching (46 IP) remains the Achilles heel | down
9. Hiroshima Radiation | 8-1 dismantling is a brutal wake-up call; Carlos Correa and Schwarber are fine but the team gave up 40 runs and only 1 category | down
10. Albuquerque Bombers | Lost 7-3 but Kyle Harrison (26 K, 1.65 ERA) was a revelation; aggressive waiver activity shows a manager fighting back | same
11. Canberra Wombats | Altuve (.577 OBP), Henderson (3 HR), and Walker (3 HR) all hit — but pitching (Seth Lugo 7.15 ERA) remains a disaster | up
12. North Correa already ranked — Buttville Skidmarks | Lost 7-3 despite solid pitching (3.19 ERA, 4 QS); offense is too passive and Reynolds' production is being wasted | down
13. Benchwarmers | Carroll was a one-man army but the roster around him is still thin; barely escaped with a 5-4 win against a struggling opponent | same
14. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A 9-0 shutout loss with zero category wins is as bad as it gets; the offense (3 HR, 13 RBI) is an emergency | down
waiver_highlights: Albuquerque Bombers | B | Added Keaton Winn, Braxton Garrett, Josh Jung, Noelvi Marte | After Kyle Harrison's breakout (26 K, 1.65 ERA), bolstering the rotation and corner infield depth shows smart roster building — Garrett and Winn give real upside if they land regular starts.

General Hospital | B+ | Added Kumar Rocker; dropped Jack Leiter, Janson Junk, Cristian Javier | Clearing out the rotation dead weight and inserting Rocker is a high-upside gamble — if he develops, this already elite pitching staff becomes nearly unbeatable.

Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | B | Added Masyn Winn, Dustin May, Ildemaro Vargas, Scott Barlow | A necessary roster overhaul after a 9-0 shutout — May adds rotation depth and Masyn Winn addresses the speed and OBP gaps, but the changes may have come a week too late.

North Correa | C+ | Added Austin Wells, Brayan Rocchio; dropped J.T. Realmuto, Brooks Lee | Upgrading at catcher is sensible but dropping Realmuto is a risk — Wells needs to earn his keep quickly on a roster already relying heavily on its top stars.

Riverside Roughnecks | C | Added Victor Vodnik; dropped Mark Vientos | A minor bullpen add that makes sense given their thin saves/holds production, but losing Vientos' power upside at corner infield is a meaningful sacrifice for a team winning on offense.
